mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is persistently above 7.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth decouples from housing construction, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained periods, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, extreme monetary accommodation compresses risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in liquidity traps and forward returns turn negative despite rates below 0.25 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails when payroll growth is driven by temporary government hiring while private sector unemployment remains stagnant, causing forward returns to underperform.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 600 thousand units, weak residential construction signals deteriorating consumer confidence and credit conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during credit-constrained regimes.
failure_condition: Housing starts drop below 600 thousand but equity markets rally due to unrelated fiscal stimulus, or the 5-day return correlation with HOUST turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP and industrial production indices reflect broad-based economic growth that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative despite positive GDP and industrial production revisions, or the signal loses predictive power during stagflationary periods.
